fixes.origin:
os.environ["RUN_TAG"] = item

cmd = "sh -c 'echo $RUN_TAG && id'"
proc = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, encoding="utf-8")
fixes.replacement:
env = dict(os.environ)
env["RUN_TAG"] = item
first = subprocess.run(["sh", "-c", "echo $RUN_TAG"], shell=False, env=env, capture_output=True, encoding="utf-8")
second = subprocess.run(["id"], shell=False, env=env, capture_output=True, encoding="utf-8")
proc = subprocess.CompletedProcess(args=["env_run"], returncode=0, stdout=(first.stdout + second.stdout), stderr=(first.stderr + second.stderr))

static_findings.message: subprocess execution may allow OS command injection if input is not controlled.
